description: Harvard Art Museums golden search cases (10): the per-museum accuracy north-star for the Harvard rows in the merged artifact. Authored 2026-07-06 against the gallery.gallerynumber:* on-view set (1,817 records at measurement time), all verified live via the Harvard Art Museums API before being written down (id, title, artist/people role=Artist, gallery all confirmed on-display). Highlight candidates were checked against sort=totalpageviews,desc: the museum's own most-viewed on-view works (van Gogh's self-portrait is #1). Tiers mirror the Met set: autocomplete = local FTS top-8; full = all-results; llm = relaxed-FTS stand-in for the interpret tier. Rothko is NOT on view in this dataset (0 gallery-assigned records) and was dropped from the plan's candidate list; Sargent's on-view works are all oil, not watercolor.

cases.note: Self-Portrait Dedicated to Paul Gauguin; #1 most-viewed on-view Harvard object, id 299843, gallery 1220
The Gare Saint-Lazare: Arrival of a Train; gallery 1220

John Singleton Copley, gallery 2240; artist + title tokens land on different columns of the same row. NOT '... portrait of john adams': the title carries no 'portrait' token and Harvard's classification/medium text has no searchable source for it, zeroing the strict-AND tier (same class of miss as V&A's 'coronation cup' case)
John Singer Sargent, gallery 2100; on-view Sargents are all oil (no watercolors currently on display)

cases.query: a dutch painter's self portrait dedicated to his friend gauguin
cases.note: descriptive phrasing for the icon; 'gauguin' + 'self' survive stopword relaxation

cases.query: an american wilderness landscape of snowy peaks with a native encampment below
cases.note: descriptive phrasing for the Bierstadt; 'mountains'/'peaks' vocabulary leap tests the relaxed-FTS stand-in
